tagline: Fresh blacktop with clean edges and no driveway drama.

hero.title: Blackline driveway sealcoating hero image
hero.alt: A driveway sealcoating worker applying a fresh black sealcoat beside an unsealed cracked asphalt section.

contact.serviceArea: Residential asphalt driveways, private lanes, and small parking pads across the west suburbs, lake roads, and nearby townships

copy.eyebrow: Driveway sealcoating timed for real weather
copy.heroText: Blackline cleans, fills small cracks, and seals tired asphalt with tidy passes that make your driveway look cared for before the next rain gets ideas.
copy.introTitle: Sealcoating is half timing, half prep.
copy.introText: We look at surface condition, temperature, cure time, and edges so the finish lands smooth instead of rushed.
copy.servicesTitle: What we check before the coat
copy.quoteTitle: Ready for the satisfying blacktop reset?
copy.quoteText: Send driveway photos, rough dimensions, crack trouble spots, and the last time it was sealed. We will reply with timing, prep notes, and a clear estimate.

services.title: Crack and edge read
services.text: We flag small cracks, crumbly edges, oil spots, and drainage spots before promising a glossy finish.

services.title: Clean-and-prime prep
services.text: Loose grit, weeds, and stains get handled so the sealcoat grips the driveway instead of skating over dirt.

services.title: Tidy sealcoat pass
services.text: Squeegee application, careful borders, and taped transitions keep the blacktop sharp without painting the sidewalk.

process.title: Send the driveway
process.text: A wide photo, a crack close-up, and rough dimensions are enough to start a real estimate.

process.title: Pick the weather window
process.text: We line up the job around dry pavement, friendly temperatures, and cure time that will not ruin your plans.

process.title: Seal, edge, and rope off
process.text: We prep, apply, clean the borders, and leave simple keep-off notes so the finish cures clean.
